display-name.lang: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry's new educational assistant, Marty, becomes a source of irritation in the classroom when he challenges Gerry's authority.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry is asked to coach the wrestling tournament and promises to bring home trophies; Simon discovers his Jewish roots; Frank breaks up with Lisa.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go to an estate sale; Mutt gives Alexis a bike; Roland asks for David's help to shop for a special item.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny convinces himself to have a partnership with Bob; David has an interview at a clothing store; Moira takes care of Alexis.

programme.title: Fresh Off the Boat
programme.desc: During a sleepover, Eddie and his friends try beer for the first time; Jessica, Emery, Evan and Honey take a road trip to Georgia.

programme.title: American Housewife
programme.desc: Katie starts her day in a bad mood; the Ottos come together to cheer her up; in an alternate reality and wearing a different sweater, Katie starts her day in a good mood, and the family comes together to cheer up Greg.

programme.title: Wings
programme.desc: Brian's ninth-grade English teacher (Peggy Lipton) tests him to see if he can make the grade.

programme.title: Wings
programme.desc: Helen's mother (Debbie Reynolds) comes to town with a check for Helen and Joe's wedding, unaware that Casey is divorced.

programme.title: Becker
programme.desc: Reggie plans to help Jake attain a lifelong goal whether he likes it or not; success goes to Linda's head when she saves a patient's life.

programme.title: Becker
programme.desc: Bob offers to get a friend to give Reggie a great deal on a new freezer -- under one condition; John acts as a sex therapist for an 80-year-old couple.

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Hawkeye falls for a Swedish doctor who arrives at the 4077th to observe combat surgery.

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: A young surgeon from Tokyo makes the doctors of the 4077th feel out of touch with new medical practices.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: Dave is eager to take on a bigger role in the community and prove himself to Calvin; Calvin's reaction makes it clear that, while the relationship between the two families has grown, the friendship between Calvin and Dave needs more time.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: When Grover gets into a fight with a bully at school, Gemma is torn between her role as principal and wanting to protect her son; Calvin encourages Marty to stand up to his own bully, only to be shocked when he learns who it is.

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Jeff helps Jennifer lift her heavy sofa, but he soon feels guilty.

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: A chance meeting with an old flame prompts Russell to right a previous wrong.

programme.title: Fresh Off the Boat
programme.desc: During a sleepover, Eddie and his friends try beer for the first time; Jessica, Emery, Evan and Honey take a road trip to Georgia.

programme.title: American Housewife
programme.desc: Katie starts her day in a bad mood; the Ottos come together to cheer her up; in an alternate reality and wearing a different sweater, Katie starts her day in a good mood, and the family comes together to cheer up Greg.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: Michael visits corporate headquarters in New York on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: When Dwight, who has a fear of public speaking, is asked to give a speech at a sales convention, he turns to Michael for help.

programme.title: Mom
programme.desc: Christy helps Jill adjust to being a new foster mom; Bonnie has trouble convincing tenants that she's a good building manager.

programme.title: Mom
programme.desc: Following the death of Bonnie's mother, Christy and Bonnie discover that she had been keeping a really big secret.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: Dave is eager to take on a bigger role in the community and prove himself to Calvin; Calvin's reaction makes it clear that, while the relationship between the two families has grown, the friendship between Calvin and Dave needs more time.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: When Grover gets into a fight with a bully at school, Gemma is torn between her role as principal and wanting to protect her son; Calvin encourages Marty to stand up to his own bully, only to be shocked when he learns who it is.

programme.title: Becker
programme.desc: Reggie plans to help Jake attain a lifelong goal whether he likes it or not; success goes to Linda's head when she saves a patient's life.

programme.title: Becker
programme.desc: Bob offers to get a friend to give Reggie a great deal on a new freezer -- under one condition; John acts as a sex therapist for an 80-year-old couple.

programme.desc: Brian's ninth-grade English teacher (Peggy Lipton) tests him to see if he can make the grade.

programme.title: Wings
programme.desc: Helen's mother (Debbie Reynolds) comes to town with a check for Helen and Joe's wedding, unaware that Casey is divorced.

programme.desc: Hawkeye falls for a Swedish doctor who arrives at the 4077th to observe combat surgery.

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: A young surgeon from Tokyo makes the doctors of the 4077th feel out of touch with new medical practices.

programme.desc: Michael visits corporate headquarters in New York on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: When Dwight, who has a fear of public speaking, is asked to give a speech at a sales convention, he turns to Michael for help.

programme.desc: Christy helps Jill adjust to being a new foster mom; Bonnie has trouble convincing tenants that she's a good building manager.

programme.title: Mom
programme.desc: Following the death of Bonnie's mother, Christy and Bonnie discover that she had been keeping a really big secret.

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Jeff helps Jennifer lift her heavy sofa, but he soon feels guilty.

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: A chance meeting with an old flame prompts Russell to right a previous wrong.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: Dave is eager to take on a bigger role in the community and prove himself to Calvin; Calvin's reaction makes it clear that, while the relationship between the two families has grown, the friendship between Calvin and Dave needs more time.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: When Grover gets into a fight with a bully at school, Gemma is torn between her role as principal and wanting to protect her son; Calvin encourages Marty to stand up to his own bully, only to be shocked when he learns who it is.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira tries to beautify the town; David misuses the company credit card; a grooming misstep forces Alexis and Mutt to question their relationship.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny considers running for town council; David and Stevie look for love; Alexis copes with being single.

programme.title: Due South
programme.desc: Kowalski and Fraser begin an investigation that reveals the dark side of boxing after one of Kowalski's students injures someone in the ring.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry sells One Direction merchandise then finds out the band is in town; the teachers look for excuses to avoid room 207A; Frank's expenditures are questioned.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry is promoted to full-time teacher and quickly realizes he has full-time responsibilities.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira tries to beautify the town; David misuses the company credit card; a grooming misstep forces Alexis and Mutt to question their relationship.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny considers running for town council; David and Stevie look for love; Alexis copes with being single.

programme.title: Due South
programme.desc: Kowalski and Fraser begin an investigation that reveals the dark side of boxing after one of Kowalski's students injures someone in the ring.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry sells One Direction merchandise then finds out the band is in town; the teachers look for excuses to avoid room 207A; Frank's expenditures are questioned.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry is promoted to full-time teacher and quickly realizes he has full-time responsibilities.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira tries to beautify the town; David misuses the company credit card; a grooming misstep forces Alexis and Mutt to question their relationship.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny considers running for town council; David and Stevie look for love; Alexis copes with being single.

programme.title: Fresh Off the Boat
programme.desc: Louis and the boys splurge on a pricey pay-per-view wrestling match while Jessica sleeps off the flu.

programme.title: American Housewife
programme.desc: Katie tries to get Nathan Fillion to attend the school's spring gala to impress the other moms.

programme.title: Wings
programme.desc: A famous astronaut (Robert Culp) comes to Nantucket to endorse Sandpiper Airlines.

programme.title: Wings
programme.desc: Antonio is held hostage by a crazed air-traffic controller (Dann Florek) on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Becker
programme.desc: Becker thinks one of his neighbors is sabotaging him by tampering with his laundry, apartment and mail.

programme.title: Becker
programme.desc: John is forced to host a barbecue for the gang after he receives a package containing expensive steaks.

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Both Charles and Klinger develop romantic liaisons while forgetting their troubles at Rosie's Bar.

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: The 4077th's evacuation to a nearby cave poses a big problem for Hawkeye, then Col. Potter becomes aware of it.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: When Tina decides it's time to make home improvements, Dave volunteers to repaint the Butler house, violating Calvin's "man code" and setting off a chain of events that impacts both families.

programme.title: The Neighborhood
programme.desc: When Dave accidentally knocks out the Butlers' power during a heat wave, Calvin and Tina spend the night at the Johnsons' home; Malcolm has some fun at Marty's expense when he crashes at his little brother's refurbished apartment.

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Audrey is upset when Jeff seems more interested in the outcome of a New York Knicks game than in her news about a big promotion; Adam and Jennifer argue over how they are spending their money.

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: When Audrey makes a quilt out of Jeff's favorite T-shirts, the gift is a bigger hit than she imagined.

programme.title: Fresh Off the Boat
programme.desc: Louis and the boys splurge on a pricey pay-per-view wrestling match while Jessica sleeps off the flu.

programme.title: American Housewife
programme.desc: Katie tries to get Nathan Fillion to attend the school's spring gala to impress the other moms.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: Michael strikes up a friendship with the 5-year-old daughter of his enemy when children visit the office.

programme.title: Cash Cab
programme.desc: Unsuspecting members of the public climbing into an apparently normal taxicab, only to find themselves on national television, with a chance to win a small fortune; hosted by Adam Growe.

programme.title: Cash Cab
programme.desc: Unsuspecting members of the public climbing into an apparently normal taxicab, only to find themselves on national television, with a chance to win a small fortune; hosted by Adam Growe.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Hank believes he can run a gumball business, but Oscar lends his help and a hidden talent from Karen helps it succeed.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brents finds a way to stop Oscar from saying the J-word, but discovers it might not have been so bad.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: Brent and the citizens of Dog River are annoyed when Lacey tries to make them eat local; Davis tries to train bees; Wanda sells homemade candles.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: Wanda and Lacey entice the residents of Dog River with clickbait texts, while Oscar sets up a bait shop across the street from Corner Gas to teach Brent a business lesson; Davis and Karen involve Hank in a training exercise with chaotic results.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene crashes Mark's college trivia night to prove how smart she is; Neville finds out the money he loaned Dan was used for something other than its intended purpose.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur meets an intoxicating stranger at a singles' dance; Carrie and Doug's romantic evening is spoiled.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug encourages Carrie to date the police officer who stopped her, hoping to get out of her fourth traffic ticket of the year and avoid a hefty increase in their car insurance.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug inherits his shift supervisor's job after an untimely death but soon misses his old responsibilities.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Ray (Ray Romano) invites Doug to play golf at an exclusive country club; Marie (Doris Roberts) teaches Carrie how to clean a house.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj designs a scavenger hunt that brings out the best -- and the worst -- in the whole gang.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon wants revenge after Amy ruins one of his favorite films; Leonard finds a way to get Penny to do anything; Stuart and Raj make online dating profiles.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's relationship is tested when she takes a job at his university; a fight with Bernadette leaves Howard sleeping on Raj's couch.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene crashes Mark's college trivia night to prove how smart she is; Neville finds out the money he loaned Dan was used for something other than its intended purpose.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene urges Ben to make some male friends as he feels anxious about turning 50; Mark asks Harris to buy him and his friends beer for their dorm room.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is overjoyed to be nominated for a "Soapie" award; engaged Monica frets when she realizes she will never experience the thrill of a new relationship again; Ross spots Chandler sneaking out of a male strip club.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey auditions for a big role which will require him to appear naked; Rachel and Phoebe plan Monica's bridal shower; Ross and Monica's attractive cousin visits and stirs the passions of all men who meet her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A college friend with whom Rachel shared an embarrassing and passionate moment shows up at Central Perk; Chandler and Ross argue for possession of Val Kilmer's tuxedo; Joey drives Monica crazy over the wedding schedule.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug tries to trick Carrie into going on a diet after he sees a photo of her overweight mother. With Jerry Stiller.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj designs a scavenger hunt that brings out the best -- and the worst -- in the whole gang.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon wants revenge after Amy ruins one of his favorite films; Leonard finds a way to get Penny to do anything; Stuart and Raj make online dating profiles.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's latest scientific breakthrough leaves him feeling like a fraud; a romantic gesture between Howard and Bernadette causes Penny to up her game with Leonard.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Professor Proton asks Leonard for help with an experiment, Sheldon retaliates by befriending Proton's rival, Bill Nye.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's relationship is tested when she takes a job at his university; a fight with Bernadette leaves Howard sleeping on Raj's couch.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene urges Ben to make some male friends as he feels anxious about turning 50; Mark asks Harris to buy him and his friends beer for their dorm room.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj designs a scavenger hunt that brings out the best -- and the worst -- in the whole gang.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon wants revenge after Amy ruins one of his favorite films; Leonard finds a way to get Penny to do anything; Stuart and Raj make online dating profiles.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's relationship is tested when she takes a job at his university; a fight with Bernadette leaves Howard sleeping on Raj's couch.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Professor Proton asks Leonard for help with an experiment, Sheldon retaliates by befriending Proton's rival, Bill Nye.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is overjoyed to be nominated for a "Soapie" award; engaged Monica frets when she realizes she will never experience the thrill of a new relationship again; Ross spots Chandler sneaking out of a male strip club.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey auditions for a big role which will require him to appear naked; Rachel and Phoebe plan Monica's bridal shower; Ross and Monica's attractive cousin visits and stirs the passions of all men who meet her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A college friend with whom Rachel shared an embarrassing and passionate moment shows up at Central Perk; Chandler and Ross argue for possession of Val Kilmer's tuxedo; Joey drives Monica crazy over the wedding schedule.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug tries to trick Carrie into going on a diet after he sees a photo of her overweight mother. With Jerry Stiller.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's latest scientific breakthrough leaves him feeling like a fraud; a romantic gesture between Howard and Bernadette causes Penny to up her game with Leonard.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Professor Proton asks Leonard for help with an experiment, Sheldon retaliates by befriending Proton's rival, Bill Nye.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene crashes Mark's college trivia night to prove how smart she is; Neville finds out the money he loaned Dan was used for something other than its intended purpose.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene urges Ben to make some male friends as he feels anxious about turning 50; Mark asks Harris to buy him and his friends beer for their dorm room.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Hank and Davis lead the fight against a call center that might come to Dog River.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Lacey is suspicious of the illegal duplication of Dog River money; Hank gets a case of pink eye, causing Brent and Wanda to panic in Corner Gas; Karen is frustrated with Davis.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: The sewer system backs up and causes chaos; Emma tries to trick Oscar into getting an updated family portrait.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: Hank and Oscar team up to build a shelter for the apocalypse and Davis, Karen and Wanda argue over who would be the best leader; Emma traps Brent and Lacey in the cellar so they can hash things out.

programme.title: Cash Cab
programme.desc: Unsuspecting members of the public climbing into an apparently normal taxicab, only to find themselves on national television, with a chance to win a small fortune; hosted by Adam Growe.

programme.title: Cash Cab
programme.desc: Unsuspecting members of the public climbing into an apparently normal taxicab, only to find themselves on national television, with a chance to win a small fortune; hosted by Adam Growe.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Hank and Davis lead the fight against a call center that might come to Dog River.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Lacey is suspicious of the illegal duplication of Dog River money; Hank gets a case of pink eye, causing Brent and Wanda to panic in Corner Gas; Karen is frustrated with Davis.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: The sewer system backs up and causes chaos; Emma tries to trick Oscar into getting an updated family portrait.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: Hank and Oscar team up to build a shelter for the apocalypse and Davis, Karen and Wanda argue over who would be the best leader; Emma traps Brent and Lacey in the cellar so they can hash things out.

programme.title: Cash Cab
programme.desc: Unsuspecting members of the public climbing into an apparently normal taxicab, only to find themselves on national television, with a chance to win a small fortune; hosted by Adam Growe.

programme.title: Cash Cab
programme.desc: Unsuspecting members of the public climbing into an apparently normal taxicab, only to find themselves on national television, with a chance to win a small fortune; hosted by Adam Growe.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Hank and Davis lead the fight against a call center that might come to Dog River.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Lacey is suspicious of the illegal duplication of Dog River money; Hank gets a case of pink eye, causing Brent and Wanda to panic in Corner Gas; Karen is frustrated with Davis.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: The sewer system backs up and causes chaos; Emma tries to trick Oscar into getting an updated family portrait.

programme.title: Corner Gas Animated
programme.desc: Hank and Oscar team up to build a shelter for the apocalypse and Davis, Karen and Wanda argue over who would be the best leader; Emma traps Brent and Lacey in the cellar so they can hash things out.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The hardware store experiences a smash-and-grab; Dan and Jackie nab one of the culprits; Beverly Rose learns a lesson in remembering loved ones who have passed while keeping their memories alive.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is overjoyed to be nominated for a "Soapie" award; engaged Monica frets when she realizes she will never experience the thrill of a new relationship again; Ross spots Chandler sneaking out of a male strip club.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey auditions for a big role which will require him to appear naked; Rachel and Phoebe plan Monica's bridal shower; Ross and Monica's attractive cousin visits and stirs the passions of all men who meet her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A college friend with whom Rachel shared an embarrassing and passionate moment shows up at Central Perk; Chandler and Ross argue for possession of Val Kilmer's tuxedo; Joey drives Monica crazy over the wedding schedule.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug tries to trick Carrie into going on a diet after he sees a photo of her overweight mother. With Jerry Stiller.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When a mistake comes to light, Sheldon makes Leonard walk a mile in his shoes; Penny decides to confront Raj's ex.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When the gang decides to spend Thanksgiving at Mrs. Wolowitz's house, Sheldon protests; Leonard and Penny deal with an error from her past.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy and Leonard try to help Sheldon deal with his shame; Raj stays with Bernadette and Howard for a week.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The hardware store experiences a smash-and-grab; Dan and Jackie nab one of the culprits; Beverly Rose learns a lesson in remembering loved ones who have passed while keeping their memories alive.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: After feeling the cafeteria staff could use some organization, Darlene seeks to be the manager of food services at Mark's college; Dan teaches housewives the basics of DIY home projects to make some extra money toward paying off the house.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Trying to ensure an ideal wedding, Monica arranges a meeting so Chandler and his estranged father can reconcile.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On the eve of the wedding, Ross searches for missing Chandler, who has doubts, while the girls distract unsuspecting Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to find Chandler with Phoebe's help; Rachel tries to hinder Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Fertility Test: Chandler and Monica's nervous visit to a fertility clinic is made even more uncomfortable by the unexpected appearance of Chandler's ex...Janice! (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler 'interview' Chandler's co worker (John Stamos) as a potential sperm donor. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang goes to Barbados for Ross's conference. Joey and Charlie break up when he realises they have nothing in common. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss: Ross and Joey both try to confess to each other about their new relationships with Charlie and Rachel. However, only one of them comes clean... (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One Where Ross is Fine: Rachel, Joey and Charlie attend a dinner party at Ross' apartment, but they fear for his state of mind when he overindulges in wine, song and laughter. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross goes to a tanning salon but fails to follow the "simple" instructions. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey struggle to make the transition from friends to lovers. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Emma's first birthday, Rachel convinces everyone to delay their plans so they can attend her party but plans go awry when a promiscuous birthday cake arrives... (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One Where Rachel's Sister Baby-sits: Rachel's self indulgent sister (Christina Applegate) visits and despite Ross' protests, Rachel lets her babysit Emma. (S10, E.

programme.title: True Lies
programme.desc: Secret agent Harry Tasker (Arnold Schwarzenegger) has his life turned upside down when he discovers his wife (Jamie Lee Curtis) might be having an affair with a used-car salesman (Bill Paxton).

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: "Hollywood Director" Colin rejigs a casino robbery scene using demonic possession and Les Misérables. Olympian Misty-May Treanor brings the muscle in a World's Strongest Women Competition. (S13, E.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: Lonely heart Heather must choose between Wayne's Chewbacca, Colin's submarine skipper and Ryan's evil disembodied head. Ryan & Colin insert random snatches of dialogue into a Titanic scene. (S13, E.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Randy is rallying the mall staff! Outside the mall, zombie-like masses are restless, and King Cartman prepares for battle with Princess Kenny - Black Friday is on! (S17, E.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: While Wendy tries to expose the widespread Photoshop fakery of the female form, Kanye West tries to prove to the world his fiancée isn't a hobbit - will either succeed?!? (S17, E.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys decide to create a company funded through Kickstarter so that they never have to work. When they take the Washington Redskins' name, the football team see red! (S18, E.

programme.title: Lee Evans: Live In Scotland
programme.desc: Lee Evans gives us a side-splitting grand finale to his 100 date sell-out UK tour. More vintage material here as he re-enacts coming home a drunken mess from the pub and Brits on package holidays.

programme.title: The Daily Show w/ Jon Stewart
programme.desc: Join The Daily Show team as they take a satirical sideswipe at the latest in news and current affairs. Emmy Award winning irreverence from Comedy Central.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Mr. Heckles dies and leaves all his belongings to Monica and Rachel, Chandler discovers eerie similarities between Heckles and him. (S2, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe reveals she married a gay Canadian ice dancer to help him get his green card. This spurs Joey and Chandler to share some of their secrets. (S2, E.

programme.title: Cruise TV With LoveitBookit
programme.desc: Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise holiday is your greatest one.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, and Q learn that a good babysitter can be hard to find, before performing some consumer research. Adam Pally and Jon Gabrus shock the losing joker with their devious punishment. (S13, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Q, Murr and Sal clean out their garages before giving truly spooky presentations. David Cross brings a sense of danger to the losing joker and makes a memory they're sure to remember. (S13, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Sal, Q and Murr show off the latest fashions for an awestruck crowd, before attempting to find true love. Special guest Brooke Shields shows the losing joker that breaking up is hard to do. (S13, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The Jokers crash into product displays at a grocery store, play hide and seek in an office and interact with restaurant customers as commanded by Bret, Q, Sal, and surprise guest Paul Rudd. (S10, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's parents ask Ray to give their son, Peter, some encouragement about his failing dating life. Ray arranges for a singles party for Peter and invites a few girls over. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra complains to Ray about leaving things around the house and accidentally throws out a letter Ray got from Muhammad Ali. To save her from Ray's wrath, Marie takes the blame. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray accidentally insults the twins' new friend at a basketball game by making a joke about his father's job. To rectify it he sees the friend's father and tries to apologize. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank adds salt to Marie's lasagna one night. Marie becomes hysterically depressed, as her cooking has always been her strongest point. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tells Robert that Amy talks too much after she chats with Ray while he tries to watch a basketball game on TV. Later that night, Robert tries to get Amy to talk less. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: To gain power over Debra, Ray keeps turning down Debra for sex. She keeps doing nice things to make him happy because she thinks that Ray doesn't find her hot anymore. (S9, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys go to Top of the Rock for some awkward interactions, and play a game of "Did They Deserve That?" at a bagel shop. This week's punishment involves minimal clothing and the cold. (S11, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang goes to Barbados for Ross's conference. Joey and Charlie break up when he realises they have nothing in common. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss: Ross and Joey both try to confess to each other about their new relationships with Charlie and Rachel. However, only one of them comes clean... (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One Where Ross is Fine: Rachel, Joey and Charlie attend a dinner party at Ross' apartment, but they fear for his state of mind when he overindulges in wine, song and laughter. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross goes to a tanning salon but fails to follow the "simple" instructions. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey struggle to make the transition from friends to lovers. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Emma's first birthday, Rachel convinces everyone to delay their plans so they can attend her party but plans go awry when a promiscuous birthday cake arrives... (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One Where Rachel's Sister Baby-sits: Rachel's self indulgent sister (Christina Applegate) visits and despite Ross' protests, Rachel lets her babysit Emma. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross applies for a paleontology grant and finds out that Charlie's ex-boyfriend, Benjamin Hobart (Greg Kinnear), reviews the applications. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With the Home Study: Monica and Chandler nervously prepare for a visit from an adoption worker, who confides that she slept with, and was then dumped, by a guy named "Joey". (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With the Late Thanksgiving: Monica prepares a fabulous Thanksgiving feast for the gang, but she becomes furious when everyone arrives late to the gathering. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Birth Mother: Monica and Chandler travel to Ohio to meet a pregnant woman, Erica (Anna Farris), who is considering them to adopt her child when it's born. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught: Rachel and Phoebe see Chandler getting into a car with another woman. After following him to the suburbs, they suspect he might be cheating. (S, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One Where The Stripper Cries: Rachel and Monica hire a male stripper (Danny DeVito) for Phoebe's bachelorette party, but when he gets there, Phoebe insults him and he starts to cry. (S10, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's big day becomes disastrous as deliveries go awry and a blizzard forces the plans to change. Meanwhile, Chandler and Ross compete to be a groomsman. (S10, E.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre returns with his award-winning show from the London Palladium. Will Bear Grylls survive Send To All? and Liam Payne performs. (S5, E.

programme.title: 8 Out Of 10 Cats - Bumper Edition
programme.desc: Enjoy hilarious highlights from Series, hosted by Jimmy Carr. Captains Sean Lock & Jon Richardson, guests incl. Jack Whitehall, Sarah Millican, Charlie Brooker & Jack Dee. (S, E.

programme.title: 8 Out Of 10 Cats - Bumper Edition
programme.desc: Panel show based on the weird world of stats & polls, hosted by Jimmy Carr. Team captains are Sean Lock & Jon Richardson; guests incl. Amir Khan, Richard Ayoade & Holly Walsh. (S12, E.

programme.title: Jimmy Carr's Am I the A******?
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, GK Barry and Jamali Maddix are sniffing out potential A******s. There's a controversial psychic, someone who ended a friendship over ketchup, and some wild revenge against an ex. (S1, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, and Q learn that a good babysitter can be hard to find, before performing some consumer research. Adam Pally and Jon Gabrus shock the losing joker with their devious punishment. (S13, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Q, Murr and Sal clean out their garages before giving truly spooky presentations. David Cross brings a sense of danger to the losing joker and makes a memory they're sure to remember. (S13, E.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: Lonely heart Heather must choose between Wayne's Chewbacca, Colin's submarine skipper and Ryan's evil disembodied head. Ryan & Colin insert random snatches of dialogue into a Titanic scene. (S13, E.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: Lonely heart Heather must choose between Wayne's Chewbacca, Colin's submarine skipper and Ryan's evil disembodied head. Ryan & Colin insert random snatches of dialogue into a Titanic scene. (S13, E.

programme.title: Spin City
programme.desc: Mike accidentally convinces a magazine reporter that he & Randall are lovers. Despite being nominated for "Activist of the Year.

programme.title: Spin City
programme.desc: Tired of Stuart's pranks, Paul and James team up to fight back. Randall ponders a run for Senator, and sends Mike and Caitlin to couples therapy to stop their bickering. (S4, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra and Marie have a big fight, Ray is struck with worry when he thinks Debra's anger will cause her to forget about their sex that night. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: T. & A.: After being chided for wearing a shoddy shirt and getting stains on it, Ray lies and tells Debra that members of the PTA called her outfit "trampy". (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Ally comes home with an "F" on her report card, Debra feels she is not paying enough attention, so she and Ray visit her math teacher. Ray is left unimpressed. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The women pressure Ray and Frank to accompany Robert to one of his therapy sessions. Instead, the men go to the tracks and it isn't long before the wives figure out their lie. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra invites her divorced parents, Warren and Lois, to the family Thanksgiving. Ray walks in on them having sex. Debra thinks that they are getting back together. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie is extra nice to Robert and Amy. Robert is happy until he discovers that Marie has been trying to manipulate him and Amy into having children. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica gets up the nerve to sing at Mike's piano bar, much to the audience's enthusiasm. Unknowingly, it's her "assets" that are getting most of the attention. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Blind Dates: Joey and Phoebe deviously arrange awful dates for both Rachel and Ross, hoping that an appreciation of their former relationship will cause them to reunite. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey develops an unusual acting method in order to impress a famous, pompous actor, Leonard (Jeff Goldblum), during an audition for an important stage role. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Boob Job: Chandler and Monica each ask Joey for a loan behind each other's backs. When Chandler learns Monica asked Joey for money, Joey says she needed it for a boob job. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Memorial Service: Ross and Chandler post false information about each other on their college alumni website, resulting in Chandler being posted as gay while Ross is dead. (S9, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After seeing Joey act out a romantic scene for Days Of Our Lives, Rachel starts to have feelings for him. (S9, E.

programme.title: The Ricky Gervais Show
programme.desc: Karl talks about his life changing kidney stones incident. A diary entry lifts the lid on Karl's trip to the Cotswolds with his girlfriend. (S2, E.

programme.title: The Ricky Gervais Show
programme.desc: Karl ponders height extremes when he learns that one of his new fans is actor Warwick Davis. Stephen entertains us with a story of his wild night out clubbing with his friends in London. (S2, E.

programme.title: Utopia
programme.desc: The arrival of a work experience student leads to unexpected Ministerial developments. Nat is invited to join a prestigious board but soon clashes with the Chair. (S4, E.

programme.title: Teachers
programme.desc: With Hot Dad being secretive and frequently leaving the house at night, Mary Louise thinks he may be a serial killer. Caroline tries to teach etiquette and manners to a pupil.(S3, E.

programme.title: Reno 911!
programme.desc: As Lt. Dangle trains his new Reno Bike Squad, Deputy Jones unveils his one-man revue of African-American History. (S8, E.

programme.title: Reno 911!
programme.desc: The deputies face an imposing foe in a tournament of lawn games. (S8, E.

programme.title: Iain Stirling's Roast The Internet
programme.desc: Host Iain Stirling is joined by Tom Allen, Desiree Burch and Vicky Pattison for a savage scroll through the most absurd corners of the internet, from Gen Z to dodgy travel trends. (S1, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra and Marie have a big fight, Ray is struck with worry when he thinks Debra's anger will cause her to forget about their sex that night. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: T. & A.: After being chided for wearing a shoddy shirt and getting stains on it, Ray lies and tells Debra that members of the PTA called her outfit "trampy". (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Ally comes home with an "F" on her report card, Debra feels she is not paying enough attention, so she and Ray visit her math teacher. Ray is left unimpressed. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The women pressure Ray and Frank to accompany Robert to one of his therapy sessions. Instead, the men go to the tracks and it isn't long before the wives figure out their lie. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra invites her divorced parents, Warren and Lois, to the family Thanksgiving. Ray walks in on them having sex. Debra thinks that they are getting back together. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie is extra nice to Robert and Amy. Robert is happy until he discovers that Marie has been trying to manipulate him and Amy into having children. (S9, E.

programme.title: Cruise TV With LoveitBookit
programme.desc: Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise holiday is your greatest one.

programme.title: Spin City
programme.desc: Mike courts a wealthy NYC socialite to fund Randall's Senate campaign, which Caitlin bankrupted. Nikki and Janelle compete to mentor a hunky intern. (S4, E.

programme.title: Spin City
programme.desc: Paul is going to appear on "Who Wants to Be a Millionaire" and recruits the other staffers in case he needs to phone a friend. (S4, E.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: In "Press Conference.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: "Party Quirks" sees Brad welcome some crazy guests, from Wayne's horny snowman to Ryan's fat parrot. The old favourite "Scenes From A Hat" features nude TV shows & odd messages in a bottle. (S13, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Mr. Heckles dies and leaves all his belongings to Monica and Rachel, Chandler discovers eerie similarities between Heckles and him. (S2, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe reveals she married a gay Canadian ice dancer to help him get his green card. This spurs Joey and Chandler to share some of their secrets. (S2, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Baby On The Bus: Chandler and Joey lose Ross's son while checking out women on the bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her Central Perk singing gig to a professional singer. (S2, E.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The One With The Lesbian Wedding: Monica caters the wedding of Ross's ex-wife and her lesbian lover. Meanwhile, Rachel's mum comes to visit with news that she is divorcing her father. (S2, E.

programme.title: Criminally Funny
programme.desc: The show that lifts the lid on the funnier side of crime. Including mayhem in a fireworks shop, traffic cone theft, and a digger driver's revenge. Joe Thomas (The Inbetweeners) narrates. (S1, E.

programme.title: Criminally Funny
programme.desc: The show that lifts the lid on the funnier side of crime. This episode we have bad delivery drivers, sleepy security guards, and Santa puts himself on the naughty list. Joe Thomas narrates. (S1, E.

programme.title: Comedy Game Night
programme.desc: Liza Tarbuck, along with team captains Sue Perkins and Guz Khan, invite a gaggle of guests round to play party games. Tonight features Kriss Akabusi, Kerry Godliman, and Andi Oliver. (S1, E.

programme.title: Takeshi's Castle Ft. Jonathan Ross
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross narrates as we prepare for thrills, spills and a whole load of soggy shorts as they encounter Flying Mushroom and Stepping Stone. (S3, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Sal, Q and Murr show off the latest fashions for an awestruck crowd, before attempting to find true love. Special guest Brooke Shields shows the losing joker that breaking up is hard to do. (S13, E.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The Jokers crash into product displays at a grocery store, play hide and seek in an office and interact with restaurant customers as commanded by Bret, Q, Sal, and surprise guest Paul Rudd. (S10, E.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: In "Press Conference.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? (USA)
programme.desc: "Party Quirks" sees Brad welcome some crazy guests, from Wayne's horny snowman to Ryan's fat parrot. The old favourite "Scenes From A Hat" features nude TV shows & odd messages in a bottle. (S13, E.

programme.title: Spin City
programme.desc: Mike courts a wealthy NYC socialite to fund Randall's Senate campaign, which Caitlin bankrupted. Nikki and Janelle compete to mentor a hunky intern. (S4, E.

programme.title: Spin City
programme.desc: Paul is going to appear on "Who Wants to Be a Millionaire" and recruits the other staffers in case he needs to phone a friend. (S4, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's parents ask Ray to give their son, Peter, some encouragement about his failing dating life. Ray arranges for a singles party for Peter and invites a few girls over. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra complains to Ray about leaving things around the house and accidentally throws out a letter Ray got from Muhammad Ali. To save her from Ray's wrath, Marie takes the blame. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray accidentally insults the twins' new friend at a basketball game by making a joke about his father's job. To rectify it he sees the friend's father and tries to apologize. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank adds salt to Marie's lasagna one night. Marie becomes hysterically depressed, as her cooking has always been her strongest point. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tells Robert that Amy talks too much after she chats with Ray while he tries to watch a basketball game on TV. Later that night, Robert tries to get Amy to talk less. (S9, E.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: To gain power over Debra, Ray keeps turning down Debra for sex. She keeps doing nice things to make him happy because she thinks that Ray doesn't find her hot anymore. (S9, E.

programme.title: South Park: The Classic Years
programme.desc: While Wendy tries to expose the widespread Photoshop fakery of the female form, Kanye West tries to prove to the world his fiancée isn't a hobbit - will either succeed?!? (S17, E.

programme.title: South Park: The Classic Years
programme.desc: Cartman dreams he has a close encounter with an alien. The other boys are convinced the dream was real, and that he has been the victim of an alien anal probe. (S1, E.

programme.title: South Park: The Classic Years
programme.desc: Cartman wins a school essay competition by cheating and decides to buff up before receiving his award from Kathy Lee Gifford. Mr. Garrison and Mr. Hat plot to kill her! (S1, E.

programme.title: South Park: The Classic Years
programme.desc: The boys go on a hunting trip with Stan's Uncle Jimbo to find the famous Scuzzlebutt. They are unaware that the mountain they are hunting on is a volcano on the verge of erupting! (S1, E.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A battle for spiritual domination conflicts with Cartman's birthday.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Government authorities mistake Cartman for a starving child.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Town denizens try for a politically correct Christmas; Kyle's pal Mr. Hankey arrives.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: While Mr. Garrison leaves the class for a visit to Tom's Rhinoplasty, Stan, Kyle, Kenny and Cartman compete for the attention of Ms. Ellen, the new substitute teacher.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A prehistoric monster threatens the world.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The school counselor suggests Cartman find his father.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman discovers his father's identity.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A crime wave hits South Park, chickens are violated, and Barbrady resigns.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Doug compete for the honor of class snack captain.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys recount their first true love stories, leading to parodies of "Castaway", "Dirty Dancing" and various Meg Ryan rom-coms.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys try to help Mayor Wild West reconnect with his estranged father, Old West.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is mistaken for being a single parent and relishes in the perks that come with it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg joins the basketball team; Stewie goes down a rabbit hole of conspiracies, which ultimately ends with him developing multiple personalities.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To restore his public image, Carter adopts a young girl from an orphanage, drawing jealousy from Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter tries to take advantage of a work policy granting leave in the event of a pet's death; Stewie causes Brian to give up writing.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois is blocked from a vacation rental app and accused of stealing; Stewie and Brian try to sell saltwater taffy door to door.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gets a job as the new night watchman, he and the guys turn Pawtucket Brewery into an after-hours bar; Stewie and Brian's plan to collect "lost dog" return money backfires when Brian is used for a dangerous TikTok stunt.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Brian's Instagram is hacked, Meg takes Stewie and Brian to Russia, only to realize she'd rather stay there; Lois reverts to her tormented teenage self when Quagmire starts dating her high school bully.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg gets married in Russia but returns home after discovering she was used for a green card marriage; Chris, in need of an extracurricular activity, joins Principal Shepherd in shooting pornos at the high school.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is lost in a mall overnight after his family abandons him; to win over a girl, Brian pretends he's a millionaire.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Chris becomes homecoming king, Meg learns that the cool kids plan to prank him during the homecoming ceremony; Peter and the guys try to replace Cleveland.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Brian falls in love with a tech support worker, he and Stewie travel to India to find her; Lois helps Joe reclaim the spotlight when Peter becomes the center of attention at bingo night.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Lois try to help Chris manage his anger by encouraging him to take up a sport; Frank Sinatra Jr., Stewie and Brian open an Italian restaurant.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the airline pilots on strike, Quagmire decides to become a gigolo to earn money; Brian gets a job at a hardware store for the health insurance.

programme.title: The Daily Show
programme.desc: A satirical look at news, trends, pop culture, current events, politics, sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is mistaken for being a single parent and relishes in the perks that come with it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg joins the basketball team; Stewie goes down a rabbit hole of conspiracies, which ultimately ends with him developing multiple personalities.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To restore his public image, Carter adopts a young girl from an orphanage, drawing jealousy from Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter tries to take advantage of a work policy granting leave in the event of a pet's death; Stewie causes Brian to give up writing.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys sign Butters up as a guest on a popular afternoon talk show that features people with weird deformities.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys see an opportunity to make money from a weight-loss program.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys' parents are offered a free ski trip to Aspen; Stan has a run in with one of the locals.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys eagerly anticipate the trailer to the new Terrance and Philip movie.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys fly to the rescue of helpless calves destined for slaughter.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Scandal impacts church attendance in South Park.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys form a club in order to stop Hollywood directors from wrecking their own films.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman gets kicked out of the gang when Bebe develops boobs and the boys lose their minds, threatening their friendships and the society.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: South Park parents go to extreme measures to protect their children from kidnappers.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: School counselor Mackey gets fired; Kyle tries to prevent Ike's circumcision.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The town's dodge-ball team goes to the championships; the school nurse deals with a strange medical disorder.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Ned and Jimbo's hunting show faces a ratings war with "Jesus and Pals.".

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: It's up to Ms. Crabtree to save the entire busload of South Park Elementary School kids after a crash.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A ban on fireworks threatens the 4th of July festivities until the mayor comes up with a plan to put South Park on the map.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A film festival comes to South Park, and the town and Mr. Hankey are overrun by Hollywood types and the expanding population.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The kids' parents have only their best interests at heart when they arrange for the kids to be exposed to the chickenpox virus.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The new planetarium has a strange pull on Stan, Kyle and Kenny; Cartman wants the lead in "Les Misérables.".

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys build rival clubhouses; Stan invites Wendy to play truth or dare.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys want to compete at bull-riding; area cows get religion.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Talented friends organize a benefit concert for penniless Chef.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Stan believes his pet fish is involved in a series of murders.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Uncle Howard escapes from the slammer to join Grandma Cartman's holiday celebration.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys are assigned a group project and end up working with a nervous geeky kid named Tweek; a cappuccino conglomerate eyes South Park.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys are sent to the Costa Rican rainforest as a part of the Getting Gay with kids choir tour; Cartman is determined to cause chaos.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The citizens of South Park are exploding randomly; Stan's Dad is on the case.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys coerce two of their reluctant classmates, Tweek and Craig, into a school yard fight.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: While camping on the banks of Starks Pond, the boys discover the last two Jakovasaurs left alive on the planet.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman files a sexual harassment suit against Stan.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A meteor shower hits South Park; Shelly invites her somewhat unsuitable boyfriend to join her when she babysits for Cartman, at the same time Cartman's cat is on heat.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The boys' call to the police creates a stand-off.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: South Park waits for the meteor shower; Kyle and Ike go camping.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Lois try to help Chris manage his anger by encouraging him to take up a sport; Frank Sinatra Jr., Stewie and Brian open an Italian restaurant.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the airline pilots on strike, Quagmire decides to become a gigolo to earn money; Brian gets a job at a hardware store for the health insurance.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Viewers are taken for a behind-the-scenes look at a "Family Guy" production week; Peter is replaced and pitches a new series to Fox Television Group Chairmen and CEO's Dana Walden and Gary Newman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Taylor Swift agrees to go to the homecoming dance with Chris; Peter decides to become an Uber driver.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois decide not to vaccinate Stewie after reading about the potential side effects; the rest of Quahog soon follows suit.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The cast re-enacts "The Great Gatsby," "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn" and "Of Mice and Men.".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter overhears Carter's plan to save money by using toxic chemicals in the brewery's beer cans, he tells a journalist about it; Brian asks for Stewie's help when his driver's license expired.
